macro_segments.title: Nazir Who Accepts Nezirut While Tamei: Initial Framework and the Dispute Between Rabbi Yochanan and Reish Lakish

micro_segments.title: Opening Case: Nezir in Cemetery and When Nezirut Takes Effect
Mishnah: Tamei Who Becomes Nazir Does Not Bring Korban; Day Seven Begins Counting
Rabbi Eliezer's View: Korban Only on Second Day of Taharah
Gemara's Initial Approach: Does Nezirut Take Effect While Tamei or Only After Taharah?
Reish Lakish's Position: Nezirut Suspended While Tamei; No Lashes for Transgressions
Tosafot Discussion: Explicit Re-Acceptance of Nezirut Needed After Becoming Tahor
Gemara's Self-Correction: Nezirut Takes Effect Immediately, Dispute Is About Severity of Transgressions

macro_segments.title: Resolution: Nezirut Takes Full Effect Even While Tamei; Mishnah Focuses on Korban Question, Not Transgressions

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan's Challenge: Thirty Days in Cemetery Should Mention Lashes If Nezirut Different
Reish Lakish's Response: Mishnah Only Addresses Korban Question, Not Transgressions
Baraita Proof: Tamei Who Becomes Nazir Prohibited and Gets Lashes for All Transgressions
Gemara Conclusion: Full Nezirut Applies; Only Difference Is Timing of Counting and Korban

macro_segments.title: Rava's Question: Whether Tarrying in Cemetery Constitutes Transgression; Distinction Between Beit Hamikdash and Outside

micro_segments.title: Framework: When Does Unintentional Tumah Become Culpable Through Continued Presence?
Beit Hamikdash Model: Staying After Warning Becomes the Transgression; Applying to Nazir
Case When Warning Was Given: Willful Acceptance Already Constitutes Transgression
Case of Ones (Unintentional): Tarrying Could Become the Act; Gemara Remains Undecided (Teiku)
Philosophical Distinction: Beit Hamikdash as Sacred Space vs Nazir as Sacred Person in Profane Space

macro_segments.title: Rav Ashi's Question: Does Nazir Starting as Tamei Require Shaving on Day Seven to Restart Nezirut?

micro_segments.title: Initial Question: Is Shaving Part of Package with Korban or Required Separately?
Mishnah Evidence: No Korban Mentioned, Therefore No Shaving
Baraita Analysis: When Reentering Cemetary, Only Difference Is Day Seven vs Day Eight
Gemara Response: Shaving Wrapped in Package with Korban and Counting

macro_segments.title: Baraita on Metzora and Nazir: Do Days as Metzora Count Toward Nezirut? Comparison via Shaving

micro_segments.title: Tannaitic Rule: Only Tumat Meit Destroys Earlier Days; Metzora Status Does Not
Baraita Attempt: Analogy Between Tumat Meit and Metzora via Shaving Requirement
First Interpretation (Rav Pappa): Shaving at Start of Taharah; Comparison of Nezirut Modes
Second Interpretation: Shaving at End of Nezirut; Hair Safety as Measure of Nezirut Strength
Conclusion: Baraita Ambiguous on Whether Nazir Tamei in Cemetery Must Shave on Day Seven

topical_tags.term: Shaving/Giluach (hair cutting upon completion or restart)
Day Seven vs Day Eight counting
Parah Adumah (red heifer ash for purification)
